title: Siddhartha Gautama Born
description: The Buddha's exact dates are debated, but he is traditionally placed in the 6th to 5th century BCE.

title: Enlightenment at Bodh Gaya
description: According to Buddhist tradition, Siddhartha attains enlightenment and becomes the Buddha.

title: First Sermon at Sarnath
description: The Buddha gives his first sermon, setting the Wheel of Dharma in motion.

title: First Sangha Forms
description: An early community of monks forms around the Buddha's teachings.

title: Buddha's Parinirvana
description: Traditional chronologies place the Buddha's death in the 5th century BCE.

title: First Buddhist Council
description: Tradition holds that an early council is convened at Rajagriha after the Buddha's death.

title: Ashoka Embraces Buddhism
description: After the Kalinga war, Mauryan emperor Ashoka becomes Buddhism's greatest early royal patron.

title: Third Buddhist Council
description: Tradition links the Third Council at Pataliputra with Ashoka's reign.

title: Buddhism Reaches Sri Lanka
description: Missionary activity during Ashoka's period carries Buddhism to Sri Lanka.

title: Mahayana Buddhism Emerges
description: Mahayana currents begin to appear in the last centuries BCE and early centuries CE.

title: Kushan Patronage
description: Under rulers such as Kanishka, Buddhism flourishes across north India and Central Asia.

title: Buddhism Expands in China
description: Buddhism becomes deeply established in China during the first centuries CE.

title: Nalanda Flourishes
description: Nalanda becomes one of the most important Buddhist monastic universities in the world.

title: Xuanzang's Pilgrimage
description: The Chinese monk Xuanzang travels to India and helps transmit Buddhist texts and ideas back to East Asia.

title: Buddhism Established in Tibet
description: Buddhism takes root in Tibet during the imperial period and later revival.

title: Zen Reaches Japan
description: Zen schools gain a major foothold in Japan during the Kamakura period.

title: Decline in Much of India
description: By the late first and early second millennium CE, Buddhism declines across much of India.

title: Modern Revival at Bodh Gaya
description: The 19th-century Buddhist revival helps renew global attention to major Buddhist sacred sites.

title: Dalai Lama Goes into Exile
description: In 1959 the 14th Dalai Lama flees Tibet, reshaping modern global awareness of Tibetan Buddhism.

title: Global Expansion in the Modern Era
description: In the 20th century, Buddhist traditions spread widely through migration, scholarship, and conversion.
